mission.title: oauth2-proxy: Support for Traefik
mission.description: Support for Traefik. Requested by 30+ users.

steps.title: Check current oauth2-proxy deployment
steps.description:
Verify your oauth2-proxy version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n oauth2-proxy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oauth2-proxy
helm list -n oauth2-proxy 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working oauth2-proxy installation.

steps.title: Review oauth2-proxy configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant oauth2-proxy configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n oauth2-proxy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oauth2-proxy
kubectl get configmap -n oauth2-proxy -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=oauth2-proxy
```
I'd like to ask if any of you has the experience to configure oauth2-proxy with Traefik? Is it supported out of the box?

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support for Traefik
steps.description:
I had a quick check through the docs and it looks like you might be able to use [Forward Authentication](https://docs.traefik.io/configuration/entrypoints/#forward-authentication)

You could check against `/oauth2/auth` the same way the Nginx `auth_request` module does, but Traefik doesn't have a
```yaml
services:
  oauth2:
    command:
    - "-cookie-secret=${OAUTH2_PROXY_COOKIE_SECRET}"
    - "-cookie-secure=false"
    - "-email-domain=*"
    - "-github-org=dispenserinc" # doesn't seem to work, see requests below
    - "-provider=github"
    - "-redirect-url=http://login.${DOMAIN}/oauth2/callback"
    - "-set-xauthrequest"
    - "-set-authorization-header"
    - "-http-address=0.0.0.0:4180"
    environment:
      - "OAUTH2_PROXY_CLIENT_ID=${GITHUB_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID}"
      - "OAUTH2_PROXY_CLIENT_SECRET=${GITHUB_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET}"
    expose:
      - "4180"
    image: quay.io/pusher/oauth2_p
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n oauth2-proxy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oauth2-proxy
kubectl get events -n oauth2-proxy --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Support for Traefik" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
I had a quick check through the docs and it looks like you might be able to use [Forward Authentication](https://docs.traefik.io/configuration/entrypoints/#forward-authentication)

You could check against `/oauth2/auth` the same way the Nginx `auth_request` module does, but Traefik doesn't have a redirection option if that request fails.
codeSnippets:
services:
  oauth2:
    command:
    - "-cookie-secret=${OAUTH2_PROXY_COOKIE_SECRET}"
    - "-cookie-secure=false"
    - "-email-domain=*"
    - "-github-org=dispenserinc" # doesn't seem to work, see requests below
    - "-provider=github"
    - "-redirect-url=http://login.${DOMAIN}/oauth2/callback"
    - "-set-xauthrequest"
    - "-set-authorization-header"
    - "-http-address=0.0.0.0:4180"
    environment:
      - "OAUTH2_PROXY_CLIENT_ID=${GITHUB_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID}"
      - "OAUTH2_PROXY_CLIENT_SECRET=${GITHUB_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET}"
    expose:
      - "4180"
    image: quay.io/pusher/oauth2_proxy:v3.1.0
    labels:
      - "traefik.frontend.rule=Host:login.${DOMAIN},${DOMAIN},PathPrefix=/oauth2"
      # this should be both login and normal domain and with the PathPrefix for the redirect

403 Permission Denied
http: named cookie not present

Sign In

and I can't continue.

Some logs from oauth2-proxy:

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with oauth2-proxy installed or the issue environment reproducible.
